Author: nxships
Name: PlayerNexusTracker
Description:
Track players you meet in FFXIV — who they are, when you ran into them, how their characters change over time. A focused social tracker built on the NexusKit framework.

Per-player detail view with Summary, Equipment, Class/Jobs, Achievements, Free Company, Encounters, History and Notes tabs. Optional enrichment from Lodestone (gear, FC link, achievement progress) and FFXIVCollect (mounts, minions, full achievement catalog). Live throttled refresh queue keeps external data fresh without hammering either service.

Configurable chat and toast notifications when players you've seen before change — rename, server transfer, Free Company join/leave/switch, achievement growth, refresh failures. Per-event filtering and history grouping so the chat doesn't drown you.

Powerful player-filter language with live preview to slice your list however you want; encounter history scoped per duty and content type; optional bridges to other Dalamud plugins (Lifestream world-travel, more to come).

Local-first: all data lives in a single SQLite file under your XIVLauncher config. Automated weekly maintenance keeps it tidy. English and German localization.
Punchline: Track every adventurer you meet in Eorzea.
FeedbackMessage:
Please send feedback over to GitHub instead of using this dialog — the form there lets you pick the right category (Bug report, Feature request, or General question via Discussions) and asks for the context we need to act on the report (plugin version, repro steps, etc).

Open the chooser at:
https://github.com/NexusFFXIV/PlayerNexusTracker/issues/new/choose

Note: text you type in this Dalamud dialog goes to the Dalamud team aggregator and does not reach the plugin author directly.

Author: KazWolfe
Name: XIVDeck
Punchline: Turn your Elgato Stream Deck into a hotbar!
Description:
This plugin allows players to use an Elgato Stream Deck as a virtual utility hot bar. Actions are called directly, meaning there is no need to set up awkward keybinds, custom macros, or any other settings.

Note that the XIVDeck Stream Deck plugin must also be installed for this plugin to be useful. You can download the Stream Deck plugin from the linked GitHub repository, under the "Releases" section.

Please report all problems to KazWolfe#2896 in the Dalamud Support server or by opening an Issue on GitHub.
TestingChangelog:
For a release that should be dedicated to pi, XIVDeck has decided to lose some weight and become quite a bit leaner. Kinda need to do that if you want to start running on phones...

- XIVDeck now properly supports Stream Deck SDK version 6.1, which brings some minor code improvements.
- A bunch of performance improvements to keep XIVDeck feeling snappy.
  - A minor optimization to hotbar monitoring means XIVDeck wastes less of each frame, every frame.
  - Removed an unnecessary cache that just made on-login and other events take slightly longer.
  - Update volume management to listen for events instead of checking every frame for volume changes.
- Update the German and French translations (thank you Alice and Khayle!).
- Adds some useful logging messages on plugin startup to help diagnose certain issues.
- Other miscellaneous fixes.

For those of you who are interested in XIVDeck (or otherwise bringing your hotbars out of the game), but don't want to buy a Stream Deck, I have some cool news. Elgato released a new version of [Stream Deck Mobile for iOS](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/elgato-stream-deck-mobile/id1440014184), which now gives you six virtual buttons free, forever. Android is allegedly going to get this update soon. Possibly. I promise I was not paid to make this statement.

Full release notes, testing notes, and downloads are available [on the plugin GitHub](https://github.com/KazWolfe/XIVDeck/releases/tag/v0.3.14).
